content.verdict: The Reykjavik Edition is the city-break bookend to an Iceland honeymoon — the Ian Schrager design hotel that finally brought capital-L luxury to downtown Reykjavík. The property sits on the old harbour, a two-minute walk from the Harpa concert hall, with 253 rooms done in the Edition's signature hushed-Scandinavian palette: cream, pale timber, Icelandic wool, black-steel accents. Street-level is a chef-driven restaurant, a lobby bar that locals actually frequent, and a ballroom that doubles as a late-night scene. Upstairs there's a serene indoor pool and spa, and on top of it all the Tides rooftop bar, with Reykjavík's best skyline-plus-harbour view. As a honeymoon base it works beautifully for a 2-3 night urban opener or closer — walking to Laugavegur shopping, coffee culture, and harbour whale-watching — paired with a rural retreat for the rest of the week.
content.best_room: Harbour View Suite — oversized window onto the old harbour and Mount Esja, king bed, soaking tub with city views, and a separate living room with a day bed for lounging.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and harbour stroll
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Keflavík (50 minutes), check-in at the Edition, and an evening walk along the harbour past the Sun Voyager sculpture before dinner at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Reykjavík design day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Harpa and Hallgrímskirkja, lunch on Laugavegur, afternoon at the Reykjavík Art Museum, and a rooftop sunset at Tides bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Blue Lagoon and spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-booked premium Blue Lagoon slot (50-minute drive each way), lunch there, and return for a couples massage at the Edition spa and a quiet hotel dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Golden Circle by private guide
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day Golden Circle with a private driver — Þingvellir, Geysir, Gullfoss, and Kerið — with a greenhouse lunch at Friðheimar and return in time for a harbour-side dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: South Coast one-day highlights
itinerary_7_nights.description: Seljalandsfoss, Skógafoss, Reynisfjara, and Sólheimajökull with a local guide, returning for late drinks at Tides.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Whale watching and food tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning whale-watching boat from the harbour right outside the hotel, an afternoon Reykjavík food tour, and a farewell tasting-menu dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow morning and departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast, one last hour at the spa and pool, and an afternoon transfer back to Keflavík for your flight home.

caveats: Reykjavik is an urban hotel — rooms are elegant but the experience is city-based; pair it with a rural property like Torfhús or Rangá for a rounded Iceland honeymoon.
The hotel does not have its own geothermal pool; the indoor pool and spa are stylish but not the 'Icelandic steam in a lava field' experience.
Downtown Reykjavík weekends can be lively into the early hours — request a higher-floor, harbour-side room if you're a light sleeper.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the urban half of a split-stay honeymoon. Romantic Harbour View Suites, a rooftop bar, walkable access to Harpa and Laugavegur, and serious restaurants make it a great city base.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June to August for the midnight sun and outdoor dining, and November to March for aurora tours from the city. May and September are quieter sweet spots.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: A Harbour View Suite, a table at the ground-floor restaurant, a pre-booked Blue Lagoon Premium slot, and Golden Circle and South Coast private tours.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, the Edition is a full-service urban hotel that welcomes all guests, though the design and price point skew toward adult travellers and couples.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Harbour View Suite is the sweet spot. The Penthouse Suite is the ultimate upgrade if the budget stretches — private terrace over the harbour and city skyline.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Keflavík (KEF); the hotel is a 50-minute ride away. The Flybus or a private transfer both drop directly at the hotel entrance.
